heading: Medicare and Medicaid program integrity provisions
label: 1320a–7k Medicare and Medicaid program integrity provisions
notes: References in TextThe Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, referred to in subsec. (a)(2), is , , . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 1996 Amendments note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 104–191110 Stat. 1936section 201 of this titleAug. 21, 1996

children.label: I
children.text: The programs under subchapters XVIII and XIX (including parts A, B, C, and D of subchapter XVIII).

children.label: II
children.text: The program under subchapter XXI.

children.label: III
children.text: Health-related programs administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.

children.label: IV
children.text: Health-related programs administered by the Secretary of Defense.

children.label: V
children.text: The program of old-age, survivors, and disability insurance benefits established under subchapter II.

children.label: VI
children.text: The Indian Health Service and the Contract Health Service program.

children.heading: Priority for inclusion of certain data
children.label: ii Priority for inclusion of certain data
children.text: Inclusion of the data described in subclause (I) of such clause in the Integrated Data Repository shall be a priority. Data described in subclauses (II) through (VI) of such clause shall be included in the Integrated Data Repository as appropriate.11So in original. Probably should be “clause (i)”.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into agreements with the individuals described in clause (ii) under which such individuals share and match data in the system of records of the respective agencies of such individuals with data in the system of records of the Department of Health and Human Services for the purpose of identifying potential fraud, waste, and abuse under the programs under subchapters XVIII and XIX.

children.label: I
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security.

children.label: II
children.text: The Secretary of Veterans Affairs.

children.label: III
children.text: The Secretary of Defense.

children.label: IV
children.text: The Director of the Indian Health Service.

children.heading: Definition of system of records
children.label: iii Definition of system of records
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “system of records” has the meaning given such term in .section 552a(a)(5) of title 5

children.heading: Access to claims and payment databases
children.label: 2 Access to claims and payment databases
children.text: For purposes of conducting law enforcement and oversight activities and to the extent consistent with applicable information, privacy, security, and disclosure laws, including the regulations promulgated under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 and , and subject to any information systems security requirements under such laws or otherwise required by the Secretary, the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services and the Attorney General shall have access to claims and payment data of the Department of Health and Human Services and its contractors related to subchapters XVIII, XIX, and XXI.section 552a of title 5

children.heading: OIG authority to obtain information
children.label: b OIG authority to obtain information
A
children.text: is a provider of medical or other items or services, supplier, grant recipient, contractor, or subcontractor; or

children.label: B
children.text: directly or indirectly provides, orders, manufactures, distributes, arranges for, prescribes, supplies, or receives medical or other items or services payable by any Federal health care program (as defined in ) regardless of how the item or service is paid for, or to whom such payment is made.section 1320a–7b(f) of this title

children.heading: Inclusion of certain information
children.label: 2 Inclusion of certain information
children.text: Information which the Inspector General may obtain under paragraph (1) includes any supporting documentation necessary to validate claims for payment or payments under subchapter XVIII or XIX, including a prescribing physician’s medical records for an individual who is prescribed an item or service which is covered under part B of subchapter XVIII, a covered part D drug (as defined in ) for which payment is made under an MA–PD plan under part C of such subchapter, or a prescription drug plan under part D of such subchapter, and any records necessary for evaluation of the economy, efficiency, and effectiveness of the programs under subchapters XVIII and XIX.section 1395w–102(e) of this title

children.heading: Administrative remedy for knowing participation by beneficiary in health care fraud scheme
children.label: c Administrative remedy for knowing participation by beneficiary in health care fraud scheme

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In addition to any other applicable remedies, if an applicable individual has knowingly participated in a Federal health care fraud offense or a conspiracy to commit a Federal health care fraud offense, the Secretary shall impose an appropriate administrative penalty commensurate with the offense or conspiracy.

children.label: A
children.text: entitled to, or enrolled for, benefits under part A of subchapter XVIII or enrolled under part B of such subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: eligible for medical assistance under a State plan under subchapter XIX or under a waiver of such plan; or

children.label: C
children.text: eligible for child health assistance under a child health plan under subchapter XXI.

children.heading: Reporting and returning of overpayments
children.label: d Reporting and returning of overpayments
A
children.text: report and return the overpayment to the Secretary, the State, an intermediary, a carrier, or a contractor, as appropriate, at the correct address; and

children.label: B
children.text: notify the Secretary, State, intermediary, carrier, or contractor to whom the overpayment was returned in writing of the reason for the overpayment.

children.heading: Deadline for reporting and returning overpayments
children.label: 2 Deadline for reporting and returning overpayments
A
children.text: the date which is 60 days after the date on which the overpayment was identified; or

children.label: B
children.text: the date any corresponding cost report is due, if applicable.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 3 Enforcement
children.text: Any overpayment retained by a person after the deadline for reporting and returning the overpayment under paragraph (2) is an obligation (as defined in ) for purposes of section 3729 of such title.section 3729(b)(3) of title 31

children.heading: Knowing and knowingly
children.label: A Knowing and knowingly
children.text: The terms “knowing” and “knowingly” have the meaning given those terms in .section 3729(b) of title 31

children.heading: Overpayment
children.label: B Overpayment
children.text: The term “overpayment” means any funds that a person receives or retains under subchapter XVIII or XIX to which the person, after applicable reconciliation, is not entitled under such subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “person” means a provider of services, supplier, medicaid managed care organization (as defined in ), Medicare Advantage organization (as defined in ), or PDP sponsor (as defined in ).section 1396b(m)(1)(A) of this titlesection 1395w–28(a)(1) of this titlesection 1395w–151(a)(13) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: ii Exclusion
children.text: Such term does not include a beneficiary.

children.heading: Inclusion of national provider identifier on all applications and claims
children.label: e Inclusion of national provider identifier on all applications and claims
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate a regulation that requires, not later than , all providers of medical or other items or services and suppliers under the programs under subchapters XVIII and XIX that qualify for a national provider identifier to include their national provider identifier on all applications to enroll in such programs and on all claims for payment submitted under such programs.January 1, 2011
